codeReviewIssues.description: vitest was inserted between typescript and vaul, breaking alphabetical catalog ordering. 'vaul' (va) should come before 'vitest' (vi). The correct insertion point is after vaul.
vitest devDependency in root package.json is unnecessary. The root only runs 'turbo test' which delegates to workspace packages that each have their own vitest devDep. Not harmful, just redundant.

sharedStateObservations.description: The --passWithNoTests flag convention is not documented. All workspace test scripts use 'vitest run --passWithNoTests' to allow packages with no test files to pass. Future workers should know this pattern exists so they don't remove it or wonder why empty packages pass.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/utils/package.json, packages/ai/package.json, apps/web/package.json all use 'vitest run --passWithNoTests'

summary: The setup-vitest feature is well-implemented and meets all expected behaviors. vitest 4.0.18 is in the pnpm catalog, vitest.workspace.ts correctly defines workspace projects for packages/utils, packages/ai, and apps/web. turbo.json has a 'test' task with cache:false and dependsOn:^build. Each package has a 'test' script using 'vitest run --passWithNoTests'. The worker verified turbo test exits 0, turbo lint exits 0, and turbo ts exits 0. Two minor non-blocking issues: catalog ordering (vitest before vaul) and unnecessary vitest devDep in root. The worker followed the infra-worker skill procedure. PASS.
